name: Celastrus Paniculatus
summary: Internal cross-linking supports Celastrus Paniculatus through compounds such as beta-amyrin, celastrine, lupeol.
description: Internal cross-linking supports Celastrus Paniculatus through compounds such as beta-amyrin, celastrine, lupeol.
contraindications: sedative or benzodiazepine medication because celastrus paniculatus exerts CNS-depressant activity and may have an additive sedative effect
pregnancy because celastrus paniculatus has traditional abortifacient and uterine-stimulant use
male fertility concerns or high-dose long-term use because animal studies have shown reversible antispermatogenic and liver effects at high doses

sources.title: Phytochemistry and pharmacology of Celastrus paniculatus Wild.: a nootropic drug
Neuroprotective potential of Celastrus paniculatus seeds against common neurological ailments: a narrative review
An extensive review on phytochemistry and pharmacological activities of Indian medicinal plant Celastrus paniculatus Willd
